nodes.label: YES proposition
nodes.text: The proposition resolves YES.

nodes.label: Qualifying objective established
nodes.text: The January 3, 2026 ground operation had at least one qualifying objective under the complaint.

nodes.label: Head-of-state edge case
nodes.text: Capture of a head of state without territorial occupation resolves NO.

nodes.label: Raid carve-out
nodes.text: Special operations raids without territorial objectives resolve NO.

nodes.label: TR-1
nodes.text: White House War Powers letter of January 5, 2026: targeted and limited military strikes in Venezuela in furtherance of apprehension and transfer, at a fortified site with expected violent resistance.

mentions.title: TR-1 — White House War Powers 48-hour report to Congress (Jan. 5, 2026)

nodes.label: TR-2
nodes.text: White House recap of January 4, 2026 remarks: U.S. forces were on the ground in Venezuela for about two hours when they went to capture Maduro.

mentions.title: TR-2 — White House recap of Secretary Rubio’s Jan. 4, 2026 remarks

nodes.label: TR-3
nodes.text: OMB Statement of Administration Policy of January 22, 2026: targeted and limited strikes in Venezuela to apprehend and transport Maduro and Flores.

mentions.title: TR-3 — Statement of Administration Policy on H.Con.Res. 68 (Jan. 22, 2026)

nodes.label: Ground entry for about two hours
nodes.text: U.S. forces were on the ground in Venezuela for about two hours during the January 3, 2026 operation.

nodes.label: Targeted and limited apprehension operation
nodes.text: The operation was framed in official Executive statements as targeted and limited action to apprehend, arrest, transfer, and transport Maduro and Flores.

nodes.label: Fortified site and violent resistance
nodes.text: The target site was heavily fortified, guarded by combat-equipped associates, and expected to resist with extreme violence.

nodes.label: Temporary site control satisfies seize-or-hold
nodes.text: Securing and holding the target site and its approaches for the duration of the raid satisfies the seize-or-hold-territory objective.

nodes.label: Capture removes the sitting government
nodes.text: Apprehending and transferring the Venezuelan head of state counts as removing the sitting government.

nodes.label: Time-limited capture raid
nodes.text: The operation was a targeted and limited capture-and-exfiltration raid rather than a territorial-control mission.

nodes.label: Temporary compound security is not occupation
nodes.text: Brief control of a fortified compound during a capture raid does not amount to territorial occupation under the edge case.

nodes.label: No qualifying territorial objective
nodes.text: The controlling sources confirm ground entry and combat posture, but they do not confirm any qualifying territorial objective.

nodes.label: C1 demonstrated
nodes.text: Temporary compound seizure meets seize-or-hold criteria, and Maduro’s capture implies government removal.

nodes.label: C2 not demonstrated
nodes.text: The operation was a time-limited capture raid that falls within the edge case and the raid carve-out, so no qualifying territorial objective is shown.

nodes.label: C3 not demonstrated
nodes.text: The controlling sources confirm entry and combat posture, but they do not confirm a qualifying territorial objective, and the carve-outs fit this fact pattern.

nodes.label: C4 demonstrated
nodes.text: Holding a heavily fortified site in the capital for two hours satisfies the seize-or-hold-territory objective.

nodes.label: C5 demonstrated
nodes.text: Capturing the head of state satisfies removing the sitting government, and the record also shows control of a fortified site during the operation.
